subject: English
chapter: Poems – A Tiger in the Zoo, The Tiger, The Panther

activities_projects.title: Comparative Character Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Compare the tigers’ personalities in all three poems.
steps: Read all three poems carefully
Identify the traits of each tiger (e.g., confined, wild, reflective)
Write a comparative paragraph highlighting similarities and differences
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will enhance analytical skills and understand nuanced character portrayal.

activities_projects.title: Theme Identification Chart
activities_projects.objective: Identify key themes like freedom, captivity, and instinct.
steps: Make a table with columns for each poem
List the major themes and provide textual examples
Present the chart in class and explain findings
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will strengthen thematic analysis skills and textual interpretation.
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of themes and examples, clarity of presentation

activities_projects.title: Symbolism Study
activities_projects.objective: Analyze symbols in the poems and their meanings.
steps: List symbols like cages, jungle, bars, stars, and pacing
Explain literal and figurative meanings of each symbol
Discuss how they reflect the tiger’s emotions and freedom
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will understand deeper literary techniques.

activities_projects.title: Mood and Emotion Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Explore how the poet conveys mood and emotion.
steps: Identify the mood of each poem
Highlight words or lines that create this mood
Explain how these moods affect the reader’s understanding of the tiger
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will connect poetic devices with emotional impact.
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of analysis and textual evidence

activities_projects.title: Diary Entry – Tiger’s Thoughts
activities_projects.objective: Develop perspective-taking and creative writing skills.
steps: Imagine being the tiger in one of the poems
Write a diary entry expressing thoughts, emotions, and desires for freedom
Include reflections on captivity vs. freedom
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will creatively explore character mindset and empathy.
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity, emotional depth, and textual connection

activities_projects.title: Discussion – Human Impact on Wildlife
activities_projects.objective: Connect poem themes to real-world issues.
steps: Identify human interventions mentioned or implied in the poems
Discuss how humans affect wild animals’ lives
Relate to wildlife conservation efforts and share examples
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will connect literature to real-life environmental awareness.
activities_projects.evaluation: Relevance, depth of reasoning, and participation

activities_projects.title: Poetic Devices Comparison
activities_projects.objective: Analyze use of literary devices in all three poems.
steps: List devices such as imagery, simile, metaphor, and alliteration
Explain how each device highlights the tiger’s feelings or situation
Compare how different poets approach the same theme using devices
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will develop analytical and comparative literary skills.
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy, depth of explanation, and comparison clarity

activities_projects.title: Creative Illustration – Freedom vs Captivity
activities_projects.objective: Visualize contrasts between wild and caged tigers.
steps: Draw the tiger in a cage and a tiger in the wild
Highlight contrasts in posture, expression, and environment
Label key elements symbolizing freedom and confinement
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will enhance creativity and understanding of theme through visuals.
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity, symbolism, and attention to detail

activities_projects.title: Poem Recitation with Expression
activities_projects.objective: Develop oral skills and interpretive understanding.
steps: Select a stanza from any of the three poems
Recite aloud with correct expression and emphasis on mood
Explain the stanza’s significance to peers after recitation
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will improve expression, fluency, and interpretive skills.

activities_projects.title: Compare Animal Emotions
activities_projects.objective: Understand emotional depth and behavioral patterns of animals in poetry.
steps: Create a chart listing emotions of tiger, panther, and tiger in zoo
Provide examples from poems to support each emotion
Discuss similarities and differences in emotional portrayal
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will enhance literary comprehension and analytical comparison skills.
activities_projects.evaluation: Completeness, accuracy, and clarity of presentation
